subject: Add tags to  main suite
commit_message:
Add tags to  main suite

when running robot, we got an overall healthcheck results, it could be
interesting to have a view on the core, dcae, multicloud, 3rparty and
API (MSB) to quickly identify the problems and eventually not run some
of the tests if it is known as not supported (e.g. dcae)
This patch does not modify the code of the existing test
It leverags robotframework tagging mechanism

Using the OPNFV functest abstraction it is trivial to run them in CI/CD,
push results to a DB and create a dashboard[1]

this patch does not modify the code of the existing test

[1]: http://testresults.opnfv.org/onap-testing/amsterdam/functest/status-heat.html

Issue-ID: TEST-80

Change-Id: I5090b8c31cc5d42ae43cc04c557a42e072e1653c
Signed-off-by: mrichomme <morgan.richomme@orange.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

you have add some tests again. Since running is based on tags, this means they will run twice.

Patch Set 1:

> you have add some tests again. Since running is based on tags, this
 > means they will run twice.

ok, I removed the tags for this file, so they should not be triggered

Patch Set 2:

but without a tag how will they be triggered as part of automation?

Patch Set 2:

> but without a tag how will they be triggered as part of automation?

the goal is not necessarily to trigger them as part as the official automation as the default file with the health tag is already doing the job.

I am using them on my lab (Orange OpenLab). 
The CI/CD chain is based on gitlab, I just triggered the run of a testing docker though this chain.
The testing docker file inherited from OPNFV which provides several possible abstractions including a Robot one (http://functest.readthedocs.io/en/latest/apidoc/functest.html).
I am using this abstraction to run the tests created in ONAP (I did not create anything new) For the moment I put the "little" robot in my repo but it is always better to rely on upstream source: the testuite repo.

Using the functest docker (for the moment on my private repo (https://hub.docker.com/r/colvert22/functest-onap/), I would like to share the code on an official repo but it sounds complex to contribute in ONAP) includes additional features such as the automatic push to a centralized database (http://testresults.opnfv.org/onap/api/v1/projects/functest/cases) and an automatic basic dashboard. 
Additionaly this docker has been optimized and "weights" 250 Mo without optimization (Still all the OPNFV tests onboarded), which is fine for CI/CD operations
I made a demo during the weekly integration meeting some weeks ago, the slide deck is here: https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Integration+Meeting+Minutes?preview=/6593670/25433913/integration_demo_E2E_chain.pdf
Change has been successfully merged by Daniel Rose
